data.title: Iran's Revolutionary Guards: Coordinated Strikes on U.S. Bases
data.description:
On March 21, local time, Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guards Corps (IRGC) announced the launch of the 70th wave of the 'Real Commitment 4' operation. According to Jin10, the IRGC stated that they conducted coordinated attacks on five U.S. military bases using Qiam and Emad missiles, along with attack drones. The targeted bases included Prince Sultan Air Base, Al Dhafra Air Base, Ali Al Salem Air Base, Erbil Air Base, and the U.S. Navy's Fifth Fleet base.

The IRGC's statement also mentioned the use of Khaibar Shekan and Qadr missiles to strike key targets, including areas in Haifa and Tel Aviv, Israel. The statement reiterated Iran's commitment to an offensive strategy and emphasized its readiness to intensify actions against any sources of aggression threatening Iran's territorial integrity and national sovereignty. The IRGC affirmed that Iran is fully prepared to confront the current phase of conflict.

data.title: Evernode CEO: XRP's Price Stagnation Linked to Retail Dominance
data.description: Evernode CEO Ashish Birla has highlighted that XRP's price is not experiencing significant gains despite increased on-chain activity. According to NS3.AI, Birla attributes this stagnation to the predominance of retail traffic in XRP's usage. He suggests that a sustainable price increase would necessitate the involvement of banks, corporations, and institutional investors utilizing XRP for working capital and as a liquidity bridge.

data.title: The United States has conditionally eased sanctions on Iranian oil for 30 days.
data.description: The United States approved a 30-day authorization on March 20, local time, conditionally easing sanctions on Iranian oil products, allowing the delivery and sale of Iranian crude oil and petroleum products already shipped as of March 20. U.S. Treasury Secretary Bessenter stated that the Treasury Department is issuing a "narrow, short-term authorization" to allow the sale of Iranian oil currently stranded at sea. By temporarily releasing existing oil supplies, the U.S. will quickly provide approximately 140 million barrels of oil to the global market. The temporary, short-term authorization is strictly limited to oil already en route. (CCTV News)

data.title: Iran Launches Missiles at Diego Garcia Base, U.S. Military Responds
data.description: Iran has reportedly launched two medium-range ballistic missiles targeting the joint U.S.-U.K. military base at Diego Garcia in the central Indian Ocean, according to The Wall Street Journal. Both missiles failed to hit the base; one experienced a mid-flight failure, while the U.S. Navy intercepted the other with a defensive missile. This incident highlights ongoing tensions in the region and the strategic importance of the Diego Garcia base for U.S. and U.K. military operations.

data.title: PRECIOUS METALS | SPDR Gold Trust Reduces Holdings by 5.144 Tons
data.description: SPDR Gold Trust, the world's largest gold ETF, has decreased its holdings by 5.144 tons, bringing the current total to 1,056.991 tons. According to Jin10, this adjustment reflects ongoing shifts in the precious metals market. The reduction in holdings may indicate changing investor sentiment or strategic repositioning within the ETF's management. Such movements are closely watched by market analysts as they can influence gold prices and broader market trends.

data.title: Harvard Professor Warns of U.S. Dollar's Declining Dominance
data.description: Harvard professor Kenneth Rogoff has highlighted a significant challenge to the U.S. dollar's global dominance. According to NS3.AI, Rogoff noted that the dollar's share of global foreign exchange reserves has decreased to 56%, marking its lowest point since 1994. He pointed out that the combination of rising Treasury yields and a weakening dollar indicates that central banks are offloading dollar assets. Rogoff also predicted the emergence of a multi-polar currency system within the next four to five years.

data.title: Bitmine staked 101,776 ETH, worth nearly $220 million.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens, Ethereum treasury company Bitmine has staked 101,776 ETH, worth $219.45 million, bringing its total staked amount to 3,142,291 ETH, worth $6.75 billion.

data.title: Trump: Considering a gradual de-escalation of military action against Iran
data.description: US President Trump posted on his Real Social Media account that as they consider gradually de-escalating major military operations against the Iranian regime in the Middle East, they are very close to achieving their established goals: 1. Completely weaken Iran's missile capabilities, launchers, and all related facilities. 2. Destroy Iran's defense industrial base. 3. Eliminate Iran's naval and air forces, including air defense systems. 4. Never allow Iran to even come close to acquiring nuclear capabilities; at the same time, the US must maintain a posture to respond swiftly and forcefully should such a situation occur. 5. Protect US allies in the Middle East, including Israel, Saudi Arabia, Qatar, the United Arab Emirates, Bahrain, Kuwait, and other countries, with the highest level of protection. Trump stated that the guarding and patrolling of the Strait of Hormuz should be undertaken by other countries using the strait, if necessary, and the US will no longer assume this responsibility. The US is willing to support these countries' operations in the Strait of Hormuz if invited, but such assistance will no longer be necessary once the Iranian threat is completely eliminated. Most importantly, for these countries, this would be an easy military operation. (CCTV News)

data.title: U.S. President Trump Initiates Emergency Oil Release to Stabilize Fuel Prices
data.description: The initial batch of oil from U.S. President Donald Trump's proposed 172 million-barrel emergency release is expected to begin distribution shortly. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the administration's efforts to manage fuel prices across the United States. This strategic move aims to mitigate potential price surges and ensure stability in the energy market. The release is part of a broader plan to address economic challenges and maintain affordability for consumers. As the oil begins to flow, the administration hopes to see a positive impact on fuel costs nationwide.

data.title: Bank of England's Stablecoin Cap Proposal Faces Criticism
data.description: The Bank of England's proposal to impose a cap on systemic sterling stablecoin holdings has sparked criticism from both industry stakeholders and political figures. According to NS3.AI, the proposed limits of £20,000 per individual and £10 million per business have been met with backlash. Critics argue that these restrictions, along with a rule requiring 40% of reserves to be held in non-interest-bearing accounts at the Bank of England, could negatively impact payment systems, the economic viability of issuers, and the UK's competitive position in the global market.

data.title: Dollar Index Steady Amid Rising Interest Rate Expectations
data.description:
The dollar index remained stable as market expectations for a potential interest rate hike by the Federal Reserve increased. According to RTHK, the index was reported at 99.5 earlier, marking a 0.2% rise, although it has declined by about 1% over the week, ending a two-week upward trend.

The U.S. dollar also strengthened against the Japanese yen, rising above 159, peaking at 159.38, and settling around 159.2 by the New York close, up approximately 1%. Commodity currencies experienced larger declines, with the Australian dollar falling to around 0.702 against the U.S. dollar, a drop of about 0.9%. Similarly, the New Zealand dollar fell over 0.6%, remaining above 0.583.

U.S. Treasury yields saw significant increases, with the 10-year yield reaching a high of 4.393%, up 11 basis points, marking the highest level in over seven and a half months. It hovered around 4.385% by the close. The 30-year yield briefly surpassed 4.96%, while the two-year yield exceeded 3.94% before retreating to 4.94% and 3.9%, respectively, by the end of the trading session.

data.title: Blackstone's Private Credit Fund Experiences First Monthly Loss in Over Three Years
data.description:
Blackstone's flagship private credit fund has recorded its first monthly loss in more than three years, signaling a downturn in the $1.8 trillion market. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting this development as a significant indicator of weakening performance in the sector. The fund's loss marks a notable shift, as it had previously maintained consistent growth despite broader economic challenges.

The private credit market, valued at $1.8 trillion, has been a robust area for investment, attracting significant interest from institutional investors seeking higher yields. However, recent economic conditions have begun to impact its stability, with Blackstone's fund loss serving as a prominent example.

This downturn in performance could have broader implications for the private credit industry, as investors reassess their strategies in light of changing market dynamics. The loss underscores the challenges faced by funds in maintaining growth amid fluctuating economic conditions.

As the market continues to evolve, stakeholders are closely monitoring developments to gauge future trends and potential impacts on investment strategies.

data.title: X-Energy Pursues U.S. IPO Amid Middle East Tensions
data.description: X-Energy has filed for an initial public offering (IPO) in the United States, marking its intention to go public despite ongoing geopolitical tensions in the Middle East. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the company's move as part of a broader trend of firms seeking public listings even as market sentiment remains cautious due to regional instability. The decision by X-Energy underscores a continued interest in capital markets, with companies navigating complex global dynamics to secure funding and growth opportunities.

data.title: Qantas Airways Confirms Fuel Supply Stability Amid Iran Conflict
data.description: Qantas Airways has confirmed that its fuel supplies are secure well into the next month, according to an internal memo from CEO Vanessa Hudson. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the airline's efforts to alleviate concerns about potential shortages due to the ongoing conflict in Iran. The assurance comes as the airline seeks to maintain operational stability and reassure passengers amid geopolitical tensions. The memo underscores Qantas's commitment to ensuring uninterrupted service despite external challenges.

data.title: Trump Administration Nears Decision on New CDC Leader
data.description: The Trump administration is close to selecting a new leader for the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC). Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the challenges the agency has faced over the past year, including numerous leadership changes and external criticisms. The decision comes at a critical time as the CDC continues to navigate public health issues. The new appointment is expected to bring stability and direction to the agency amid ongoing challenges.

data.title: Colombia Plans to Profitably Exit Swiss Franc Swap Before Presidential Election
data.description: Colombia is set to conclude its contentious Swiss franc swap with a profit before the upcoming presidential election in May. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that this move signifies the conclusion of a significant aspect of the country's unconventional debt strategy. The decision comes as Colombia aims to optimize its financial position ahead of the election period.

data.title: Kalshi Achieves $22 Billion Valuation in Latest Funding Round
data.description: Kalshi has reportedly reached a valuation of $22 billion following a $1 billion funding round led by Coatue Management. According to NS3.AI, this valuation positions Kalshi slightly ahead of Polymarket, as both companies were previously seeking $20 billion valuations earlier in March. Despite this financial milestone, Kalshi is currently facing over 20 lawsuits concerning its legal status.

data.title: Middle East Tensions Drive Oil Prices Higher
data.description:
Oil prices have surged amid escalating tensions in the Middle East. According to RTHK, the May contract for London Brent crude has risen for four consecutive days, reaching over $113 per barrel and closing at $112.19, marking an increase of approximately 3.3%. Over the week, it has accumulated a rise of about 9%, continuing a five-week upward trend.

In contrast, the April contract for New York crude oil failed to surpass the $100 mark, peaking at $99.67 per barrel and closing at $98.32, which is an increase of roughly 2.3%. However, it has seen a weekly decline of 0.4%, ending a four-week streak of gains.

data.title: Hong Kong ADRs Experience Significant Decline
data.description: Hong Kong's American Depositary Receipts (ADRs) have seen a notable decrease in value. According to RTHK, ADRs for HSBC and Alibaba fell over 3% compared to their closing prices in Hong Kong yesterday, with conversions to Hong Kong dollars showing values of 120.5 and 119.8 respectively. ADRs for Hong Kong Exchanges and Clearing and AIA Group also dropped nearly 3%, converting to 385.6 and 83.6 Hong Kong dollars respectively. Additionally, ADRs for JD Group, Xiaomi, Tencent, and Meituan fell by approximately 2% or more. ADRs for major banks such as China Construction Bank, Bank of China, Industrial and Commercial Bank of China, and Ping An Insurance also experienced declines of nearly 2% or more.

data.title: JPMorgan Lowers S&P 500 Price Target Amid Middle East Conflict
data.description: JPMorgan strategists have revised their price target for the S&P 500 Index, citing limited upside potential for risk assets due to ongoing conflict in the Middle East. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the bank's concerns over geopolitical tensions impacting market dynamics. The strategists noted that the war in the region poses significant risks to global markets, potentially affecting investor sentiment and asset valuations. This adjustment reflects JPMorgan's cautious outlook on the equity market amid heightened geopolitical uncertainties.
